hidden.description: Keep going. This trophy reveals itself when earned.

alcohol-acute-survival-bronze.title: Through the storm
alcohol-acute-survival-bronze.description: The acute withdrawal phase — the most physiologically dangerous of any drug — is breaking. GABA-A receptors, downregulated by chronic ethanol, begin recovering. Glutamatergic rebound subsides. Tremor, anxiety, and the worst of it are behind you. The hardest 72 hours have passed.

alcohol-liver-bronze-1.title: First steps in Mordor
alcohol-liver-bronze-1.description: Hepatic enzyme markers (AST, ALT, GGT) begin descending from chronic-use peaks. The liver registers the change within days.

alcohol-vta-bronze.title: The first dopaminergic flicker
alcohol-vta-bronze.description: The reward circuit, flattened by chronic GABAergic suppression and dopamine downregulation, registers its first natural signals. Anhedonia hasn't lifted yet — but the hardware is starting to respond again.

alcohol-liver-bronze-2.title: Steatosis on the retreat
alcohol-liver-bronze-2.description: Hepatic steatosis begins reversing within the first two weeks of abstinence. Lipid deposits are metabolized and hepatocyte function improves measurably.

alcohol-prefrontal-bronze.title: Tony Stark's brain warming up
alcohol-prefrontal-bronze.description: Early signs of cortical recovery. Volumetric markers begin trending upward. Working memory and attention show measurable improvement.

alcohol-heart-bronze.title: Holiday heart, dismissed
alcohol-heart-bronze.description: Alcohol-induced hypertension begins normalizing. Atrial fibrillation episodes — the 'holiday heart' phenomenon — drop in frequency. The myocardium stops being chronically stressed.

alcohol-stomach-bronze.title: No more burning at dawn
alcohol-stomach-bronze.description: Gastric mucosa healing accelerates. Acid hypersecretion subsides as parietal cell stimulation ends. Erosive gastritis lesions begin to close.

alcohol-pancreas-bronze.title: Pancreas off the operating table
alcohol-pancreas-bronze.description: Acinar cells recover from chronic toxic stress. Inflammatory response subsides. Digestive enzyme production gradually re-stabilizes.

alcohol-sleep-bronze.title: REM sleep returns
alcohol-sleep-bronze.description: Chronic alcohol use suppresses REM and fragments slow-wave sleep. As the system clears, REM rebounds — sometimes with vivid dreams — and deep sleep architecture begins reassembling. Mornings start feeling like rest.

alcohol-vta-silver.title: The reward circuit reboots
alcohol-vta-silver.description: Dopaminergic signaling begins recovering from chronic suppression. Natural rewards start cutting through the noise.

alcohol-prefrontal-silver.title: Charles Xavier doesn't run me anymore
alcohol-prefrontal-silver.description: Gray matter volume in the prefrontal cortex is increasing substantially. Executive function, impulse control, and decision-making improve. You're driving the bus again.

alcohol-liver-silver.title: The liver remembers how
alcohol-liver-silver.description: Transaminase levels approach normal ranges. Liver fat content is substantially reduced. Hepatic regenerative capacity — among the body's strongest — is doing its work.

alcohol-hippocampus-silver.title: The blackouts have a counterargument
alcohol-hippocampus-silver.description: Hippocampal volume recovers measurably. Episodic memory encoding stabilizes. The neural substrate that alcohol most reliably broke — the one behind blackouts and lost evenings — is rebuilding itself.

alcohol-heart-silver.title: Cardiac muscle, recovering
alcohol-heart-silver.description: Alcoholic cardiomyopathy reverses substantially with sustained abstinence. Ejection fraction improves, ventricular dilation regresses. The heart, given enough time, is one of the better forgivers.

alcohol-prefrontal-silver-2.title: Sustained executive function
alcohol-prefrontal-silver-2.description: Prefrontal recovery is no longer episodic — it's the new baseline. Planning, abstraction, working memory operate without the chronic noise. The cortical referee no longer needs days to recover from each weekend.

alcohol-pancreas-silver.title: Insulin in its place
alcohol-pancreas-silver.description: Pancreatic exocrine and endocrine functions normalize. Insulin sensitivity improves if not chronically compromised.

alcohol-stomach-silver.title: Iron stomach restored
alcohol-stomach-silver.description: Gastric mucosa is fully regenerated. The 3-7 day epithelial turnover has done its job. Acid balance and digestive function are back to baseline.

alcohol-hippocampus-gold.title: Memory, fully on the record
alcohol-hippocampus-gold.description: Hippocampal volume and function approach baseline. Long-term memory consolidation, spatial navigation, and contextual learning operate at full capacity. Years of nights you couldn't remember — the brain has learned how to keep again.

alcohol-liver-gold.title: Better than Wolverine's regeneration
alcohol-liver-gold.description: The liver has regenerated most of its functional tissue. Mild fibrosis, if present, has partially reversed. Few organs in the body could pull this off.

alcohol-vta-gold.title: Pleasure is no longer rented
alcohol-vta-gold.description: Dopamine receptor density approaches baseline. Natural rewards feel meaningful again, anhedonia substantially resolves. The brain has remembered how to celebrate without help.

alcohol-sovereignty-platinum.title: Every system, returned
alcohol-sovereignty-platinum.description: Liver, heart, pancreas, stomach, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, mesolimbic axis, sleep architecture — every system alcohol touched runs on its own signals now. The most physiologically punishing substance to leave is fully behind you. The body is yours again, all of it.

nicotine-bronchi-bronze-1.title: Cilia stretching after a long sleep
nicotine-bronchi-bronze-1.description: Within 24-72 hours of cessation, paralyzed bronchial cilia begin to recover their rhythmic motion. The mucociliary escalator wakes up.

nicotine-bronchi-bronze-2.title: The chimney is being swept
nicotine-bronchi-bronze-2.description: Bronchial cilia clear accumulated tar and particulates with measurable efficiency. Productive cough in this phase is the system expelling residue — not a problem, but the solution.

nicotine-bronchi-silver.title: Breathing without paying tolls
nicotine-bronchi-silver.description: Bronchial mucosa has largely recovered. Chronic cough subsides, exercise tolerance noticeably improves. Each breath costs less.

nicotine-lungs-bronze.title: Macrophages on cleaning shift
nicotine-lungs-bronze.description: Alveolar macrophages, overloaded with tar particulates, intensify their clearing work. Diffusion capacity begins to climb.

nicotine-lungs-silver.title: FEV1 above the dignity threshold
nicotine-lungs-silver.description: Forced expiratory volume is improving. The rate of age-related lung function decline has normalized to that of a non-smoker.

nicotine-lungs-gold.title: Black Bolt's lungs
nicotine-lungs-gold.description: Pulmonary capacity approaches non-smoker baseline. Persistent emphysema, if present, no longer progresses. The lungs work near their full capacity again.

nicotine-heart-bronze.title: Endothelium remembers how to relax
nicotine-heart-bronze.description: Vasoconstriction induced by nicotine resolves. Endothelial function begins recovering. Acute cardiovascular risk drops with each tobacco-free day.

nicotine-heart-silver.title: Resting pulse, real rest
nicotine-heart-silver.description: Resting heart rate normalizes. Carbon monoxide is no longer competing with oxygen. Hemoglobin returns to its actual job.

nicotine-heart-gold.title: Cardiovascular risk slashed in half
nicotine-heart-gold.description: Risk of myocardial infarction drops dramatically. Platelet aggregation, endothelial function, and lipid profiles approach non-smoker ranges.

nicotine-vta-bronze.title: The nicotine alarm gets quieter
nicotine-vta-bronze.description: Upregulated nicotinic acetylcholine receptors begin returning to normal density. Cravings, when they arrive, hit fewer receptors.

nicotine-vta-silver.title: The brain stops asking
nicotine-vta-silver.description: nAChR density approaches baseline. Craving intensity and frequency drop substantially. The neurochemical demand for nicotine is dissolving.

nicotine-vta-gold.title: The reward circuit finally free
nicotine-vta-gold.description: Dopamine release in response to natural rewards normalizes. The brain no longer depends on nicotine to trigger pleasure responses. The hijacking is over.

nicotine-sovereignty-platinum.title: The chimney is gone
nicotine-sovereignty-platinum.description: Lungs, bronchi, heart, vasculature, mesolimbic axis — every system nicotine and tobacco smoke touched runs on its own signals now. nAChR density at baseline. Cardiovascular and pulmonary risk profiles indistinguishable from never-smokers. The most lethal legal addiction is fully behind you.

cocaine-vta-bronze-1.title: The first pixel of color
cocaine-vta-bronze-1.description: The reward system begins clearing the noise. D2 receptors, drowned by chronic excess, detect the first flickers of natural dopamine.

cocaine-heart-bronze-1.title: Stabilizing the arc reactor
cocaine-heart-bronze-1.description: The heart stops being a bomb about to go off. Resting heart rate and blood pressure leave the combat zone.

cocaine-heart-bronze-2.title: Coronary truce
cocaine-heart-bronze-2.description: Chronic vasoconstriction eases across the coronary tree. The arteries are no longer in permanent spasm; blood flows without fear again.

cocaine-bronchi-bronze-1.title: Lower airway cleared
cocaine-bronchi-bronze-1.description: Bronchial epithelium recovers from chronic inflammation caused by inhaled vapors and aspirated irritants. The mucociliary escalator regains its rhythm.

cocaine-vta-bronze-2.title: Home-grown dopamine
cocaine-vta-bronze-2.description: The mesolimbic axis starts manufacturing its own pleasure again. Deep anhedonia begins to crack. Welcome back to the real world.

cocaine-vta-bronze-3.title: Tonic firing returns
cocaine-vta-bronze-3.description: Dopaminergic neurons in the VTA recover their baseline tonic firing. The system is no longer locked between famine and bingeing — quiet, steady signaling resumes.

cocaine-heart-bronze-3.title: Color returns to the periphery
cocaine-heart-bronze-3.description: Cutaneous and peripheral microcirculation recover. The pallor lifts as systemic vasoconstriction releases — the cardiovascular system as a whole is letting go of its grip.

cocaine-prefrontal-silver-1.title: The Night's Watch stands down
cocaine-prefrontal-silver-1.description: The prefrontal cortex regains top-down control over limbic noradrenergic drive. Constant hypervigilance and paranoia give way to a deeper calmness.

cocaine-heart-silver-1.title: Adamantium heart
cocaine-heart-silver-1.description: The risk of lethal arrhythmias and sudden death plummets. Cardiac tissue regains its natural and stable rhythm.

cocaine-prefrontal-silver-2.title: The architect takes charge
cocaine-prefrontal-silver-2.description: The prefrontal cortex regains control over impulse. You're no longer a passenger; you're the pilot again.

cocaine-vta-silver-1.title: Rebellion in the accumbens
cocaine-vta-silver-1.description: The neurochemical hijacking has failed. The mesolimbic axis now responds to music and human contact, not powder.

cocaine-prefrontal-silver-3.title: The referee returns to the ring
cocaine-prefrontal-silver-3.description: Top-down inhibitory control from prefrontal cortex onto limbic circuits recalibrates. Reactive anxiety diminishes as the cortical brake comes back online.

cocaine-prefrontal-silver-4.title: The simulation is running
cocaine-prefrontal-silver-4.description: Enhanced ability to project the future. The brain plans long-term again without the urgency of the chemical 'now'.

cocaine-heart-silver-2.title: Restoring the vital pulse
cocaine-heart-silver-2.description: Vascular response synchronizes. Circulatory health allows the body to respond efficiently to physical effort.

cocaine-heart-silver-3.title: Deep endothelial cleansing
cocaine-heart-silver-3.description: Arterial elasticity is substantially restored. The cardiovascular system works with a fluidity that seemed lost.

cocaine-hippocampus-gold-1.title: Neuronal Lazarus protocol
cocaine-hippocampus-gold-1.description: The hippocampus records deep cellular recovery. Neurogenesis in the dentate gyrus resumes; brain plasticity is at its peak, rebuilding what was damaged.

cocaine-vta-gold-1.title: Dopaminergic sovereignty
cocaine-vta-gold-1.description: D2 receptor density normalized. You've regained the ability to feel happiness without paying a toll of exhaustion.

cocaine-heart-gold-1.title: Vibranium mesh
cocaine-heart-gold-1.description: Cardiovascular health optimized. Structural damage is minimized, and the circulatory system is at full capacity.

cocaine-liver-gold-1.title: The fortress rebuilt
cocaine-liver-gold-1.description: Hepatic detoxification machinery is back at full power. Glutathione reserves replenished, reactive metabolites of cocaine (norcocaine, N-hydroxynorcocaine) no longer overwhelming the system. Oxidative stress defeated by cellular regeneration.

cocaine-sovereignty-platinum.title: The dictator has fallen
cocaine-sovereignty-platinum.description: Absolute neurochemical freedom. The reward circuit is fully restored. You are the sole owner of your biology.

cannabis-vta-bronze-1.title: The fog begins to lift
cannabis-vta-bronze-1.description: The acute neurochemical cloud of withdrawal starts dissolving. CB1 receptors, downregulated by chronic THC saturation, begin the slow climb back toward baseline density.

cannabis-prefrontal-bronze-1.title: The first quiet morning
cannabis-prefrontal-bronze-1.description: Withdrawal-driven anxiety, irritability and emotional reactivity start to ease. The prefrontal cortex regains a sliver of top-down control over limbic noise. The first week is the steepest — and you're climbing it.

cannabis-bronchi-bronze.title: Combustion residue clearing
cannabis-bronchi-bronze.description: Bronchial cilia, suppressed by smoke and tar irritation, restart their rhythmic clearance. Productive cough in this phase is mucociliary cleanup — not regression, but progress.

cannabis-hippocampus-bronze.title: Working memory comes back online
cannabis-hippocampus-bronze.description: Short-term memory and verbal recall improve measurably as hippocampal CB1 signaling renormalizes. The 'where did I put my keys' fog thins.

cannabis-vta-bronze-2.title: Motivation has a pulse
cannabis-vta-bronze-2.description: The amotivational fog starts breaking. Dopaminergic tone in the mesolimbic axis recovers enough that goals stop feeling abstract. Wanting comes back before liking.

cannabis-prefrontal-silver-1.title: The pilot is sober
cannabis-prefrontal-silver-1.description: Executive function, decision-making and impulse control are substantially restored. The cortical referee is back in the ring; the limbic system no longer wins by default.

cannabis-hippocampus-silver.title: Long-term storage online
cannabis-hippocampus-silver.description: Memory consolidation from short-term to long-term recovers. Learning sticks. The hippocampal CB1 system, dense and central to encoding, is finding its baseline.

cannabis-bronchi-silver.title: Lungs without the rattle
cannabis-bronchi-silver.description: Chronic bronchial inflammation has substantially resolved. Exercise tolerance climbs, productive cough subsides, breathing no longer carries a backlog.

cannabis-prefrontal-silver-2.title: Emotional weather, not emotional climate
cannabis-prefrontal-silver-2.description: Sustained regulation of mood and affect. Reactive irritability and emotional flatness give way to range — feelings come and go without overwhelming the system.

cannabis-vta-silver-1.title: CB1 receptors rebuilding
cannabis-vta-silver-1.description: [11C]OMAR PET imaging would now show CB1 receptor density approaching control levels in the mesolimbic axis. Most of the four-week recovery window is behind you. The endocannabinoid system is finding its own rhythm.

cannabis-vta-silver-2.title: Anhedonia, dismissed
cannabis-vta-silver-2.description: Natural rewards land with full weight again. Music, food, sex, conversation — the dopaminergic response to ordinary pleasure is no longer dampened. The amotivational syndrome is functionally resolved.

cannabis-hippocampus-gold.title: The library is fully indexed
cannabis-hippocampus-gold.description: Hippocampal volume and function approach baseline. Memory encoding, spatial navigation and contextual learning operate at full capacity. The brain remembers how to remember.

cannabis-prefrontal-gold.title: Executive suite restored
cannabis-prefrontal-gold.description: Prefrontal cortex function at near-baseline. Working memory, planning, abstract reasoning, impulse control — all the things THC quietly dimmed for years — operating at full bandwidth.

cannabis-vta-gold.title: Endocannabinoid sovereignty
cannabis-vta-gold.description: CB1 receptor density and endogenous cannabinoid tone (anandamide, 2-AG) normalized. The body's own cannabinoid system runs the show — no exogenous override required.

cannabis-sovereignty-platinum.title: The haze has lifted, for good
cannabis-sovereignty-platinum.description: Complete recalibration of the endocannabinoid system. Reward, memory, emotion, motivation, breath — every system the cannabis touched runs on its own signals now. The body is yours, undivided.

caffeine-vta-bronze-1.title: The headache breaks
caffeine-vta-bronze-1.description: Cerebral vasodilation rebound — the cardinal withdrawal symptom — resolves as adenosine A2A signaling on cerebral vasculature recalibrates. The first 2-9 days are the steepest, and you're through them.

caffeine-vta-bronze-2.title: Adenosine receptors recalibrating
caffeine-vta-bronze-2.description: Adenosine A2A receptors, downregulated to compensate for chronic blockade by caffeine, begin restoring baseline density. Sleep pressure and natural alertness cycles are renormalizing.

caffeine-heart-bronze.title: Resting cardiovascular tone normalized
caffeine-heart-bronze.description: Resting heart rate and blood pressure return to non-caffeinated baseline. Palpitations and caffeine-induced ectopic beats subside.

caffeine-stomach-bronze.title: Gastric acid balance restored
caffeine-stomach-bronze.description: Basal gastric acid secretion returns to normal range. Reflux and dyspepsia symptoms attributable to caffeine resolve.

caffeine-vta-silver-1.title: Slow-wave sleep, finally
caffeine-vta-silver-1.description: Deep sleep architecture recovers. The suppression of slow-wave sleep that caffeine causes — even 12 hours after the last dose — lifts. Mornings start feeling like mornings.

caffeine-vta-silver-2.title: Adenosinergic balance restored
caffeine-vta-silver-2.description: Adenosine receptor density has substantially normalized. Natural sleep onset, daytime alertness, and morning energy no longer require external stimulation.

caffeine-vta-silver-3.title: Energy without the cliff
caffeine-vta-silver-3.description: The caffeine-crash-caffeine cycle is broken. Alertness rises and falls on its own circadian curve, not on the timing of your last cup. The 3pm wall stops existing.

caffeine-vta-silver-4.title: Sensitivity returned
caffeine-vta-silver-4.description: Adenosine receptor density at baseline. A single cup now hits the way it used to before tolerance built up — caffeine is back to being a tool, not a maintenance dose.
